creator: gswitkow@gmail.com
text:
Problem still occurs. 

Bunch of required details:

uname -a
FreeBSD (...) 14.1-RELEASE-p5 FreeBSD 14.1-RELEASE-p5 GENERIC arm64
OpenVPN: 2.6.12 installed from pkgs.
Platform: Raspberry Pi 3B+ (but tested on RPI 4 with the same result)

config:

port 1194
proto udp
dev tun
ca   ca.crt
cert server.crt
key  server.key
dh   dh.pem
data-ciphers AES-256-GCM:AES-128-GCM:?CHACHA20-POLY1305
topology subnet
server 10.a.b.0 255.255.255.0
ifconfig-pool-persist ipp.txt
push "route 172.x.y.z 255.255.255.0"
keepalive 5 60
max-clients 5
user openvpn
group openvpn
persist-key
persist-tun
status openvpn-status.log
verb 3
explicit-exit-notify 1
auth SHA256
tls-server
tls-version-min 1.2
tls-cipher TLS-ECDHE-ECDSA-WITH-AES-128-GCM-SHA256
tls-crypt shared.key
cipher AES-256-GCM

Result:
Start server:

2024-11-26 14:24:03 OpenVPN 2.6.12 aarch64-portbld-freebsd14.1 [SSL (OpenSSL)] [LZO] [LZ4] [PKCS11] [MH/RECVDA] [AEAD] [DCO]
2024-11-26 14:24:03 library versions: OpenSSL 3.0.13 30 Jan 2024, LZO 2.10
2024-11-26 14:24:03 DCO version: FreeBSD 14.1-RELEASE-p5 GENERIC
2024-11-26 14:24:03 Diffie-Hellman initialized with 4096 bit key
2024-11-26 14:24:03 DCO device tun0 opened
2024-11-26 14:24:03 /sbin/ifconfig tun0 10.a.b.c/24 mtu 1500 up
2024-11-26 14:24:03 Could not determine IPv4/IPv6 protocol. Using AF_INET6
2024-11-26 14:24:03 Socket Buffers: R=[42080->42080] S=[9216->9216]
2024-11-26 14:24:03 setsockopt(IPV6_V6ONLY=0)
2024-11-26 14:24:03 UDPv6 link local (bound): [AF_INET6][undef]:1194
2024-11-26 14:24:03 UDPv6 link remote: [AF_UNSPEC]
2024-11-26 14:24:03 Unable to retain capabilities
2024-11-26 14:24:03 GID set to openvpn
2024-11-26 14:24:03 UID set to openvpn
2024-11-26 14:24:03 MULTI: multi_init called, r=256 v=256
2024-11-26 14:24:03 IFCONFIG POOL IPv4: base=10.a.b.c size=253
2024-11-26 14:24:03 IFCONFIG POOL LIST
2024-11-26 14:24:03 Initialization Sequence Completed
2024-11-26 14:24:03 Failed to poll for packets: Operation not permitted (errno=1)
2024-11-26 14:24:13 Failed to get peer stats: Operation not permitted (errno=1)
2024-11-26 14:24:13 Failed to poll for packets: Operation not permitted (errno=1)
2024-11-26 14:24:23 Failed to poll for packets: Operation not permitted (errno=1)
2024-11-26 14:24:33 Failed to poll for packets: Operation not permitted (errno=1)
2024-11-26 14:24:43 Failed to poll for packets: Operation not permitted (errno=1)

Notice: "Operation not permitted" message every 10secs.  

tun0 iface is up:

tun0: flags=1008003<UP,BROADCAST,MULTICAST,LOWER_UP> metric 0 mtu 1500
        options=80000<LINKSTATE>
        inet 10.a.b.1 netmask 0xffffff00 broadcast 10.a.b.255
        groups: openvpn
        nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>

Stop server:

^C2024-11-26 14:24:44 event_wait : Interrupted system call (fd=-1,code=4)
2024-11-26 14:24:44 Failed to poll for packets: Operation not permitted (errno=1)
2024-11-26 14:24:46 Failed to poll for packets: Operation not permitted (errno=1)
2024-11-26 14:24:46 Closing DCO interface
2024-11-26 14:24:46 /sbin/ifconfig tun0 10.a.b.1 -alias
ifconfig: ioctl (SIOCDIFADDR): Operation not permitted
2024-11-26 14:24:46 FreeBSD ip addr del failed: external program exited with error status: 1
2024-11-26 14:24:46 /sbin/ifconfig tun0 destroy
ifconfig: SIOCIFDESTROY: Operation not permitted
2024-11-26 14:24:46 FreeBSD 'destroy tun interface' failed (non-critical): external program exited with error status: 1
2024-11-26 14:24:46 SIGINT[hard,] received, process exiting

Notice: "Operation not permitted". 
Process "openvpn" doesn't exist anymore, but tun0 device is still up. It needs to be shut manually (ifconfig tun0 destroy or kldunload if_ovpn). 

When I run server with --disable-dco, "Failed to poll..." messages doesn't occur, but still occurs problem with destroying tun0 device. 
When I run server as root (comment out user/group directives), there is no problem at all. Except, that one the server runs with root privileges.

For testing purposes I added user: openvpn to wheel group (dev files are wheel-grouped) but it didn't succeed. 

There is no errors with dco and device tun0 shuts down correctly on Linux box (Alpine) with the same config so i guess the problem is related to FreeBSD environment. 

Thanks for yours attention. 

Regards
GŚ.
